join_order.notes: Keep adsh_to_rowid as the cheapest first filter on the num scan.

join_order.build: pre(indexed by adsh_tag_version_sorted, filtered at probe time to stmt = 'EQ')
join_order.notes: Retain the existing binary-search plus contiguous-run probe into the sorted pre rowid index; do not build a runtime pre hash table.
Probe only after sub and pre filtering so tlabel is fetched only for surviving rows.

data_structures.dim_filter: direct_array(sub/indexes/adsh_to_rowid.bin) with row-level reads of sub.sic and sub.cik
data_structures.fact_join: pre/indexes/adsh_tag_version.rowids.bin for binary-search + contiguous-run stmt counting, plus pre-built tag_version_hash for (tag,version) lookup
data_structures.aggregation: scan-time radix/hash partition buffers on final group_key=(sic,tlabel) carrying {group_key,cik,scaled_value,pre_count}; then per-partition compact_hash_map for sums/counts plus per-partition distinct_pair_set(group_key,cik)

indexes.purpose: skip num blocks whose uom range cannot contain usd_code
direct adsh lookup before sic and cik reads
binary-search (adsh,tag,version) and scan only the matching contiguous rowid run instead of building a runtime pre hash table
lookup tag rowid and tlabel after sub and pre have already filtered the probe stream

optimization_notes: Root cause: after the iteration-1 join fix, the remaining gap is no longer in join probing but in aggregation state layout; the thread-local group map plus thread-local distinct set are replicated across 64 threads, and their combined footprint exceeds the 88 MB LLC before growth, so the post-scan merge becomes the dominant cache-miss-heavy phase. Strategy category B: keep the proven sub->pre->tag join order, but replace replicated thread-local aggregation with scan-time partitioning of qualifying rows by final group key and aggregate one cache-sized partition at a time, using per-partition sum/count and distinct-(group_key,cik) structures so dedup and merge happen locally instead of through one large 64-way merge.
